file.content:
##
## Production server config (wip)
## You probably want to run:
## export COMPOSE_FILE="compose.yaml:compose.production.yaml"
## docker compose up -d
##

services:
  web:
    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 30 --timeout 300 --max-requests 1500
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      # Note this won't work with uv pip, because uv makes a network request
      - BEFORE_START=pip install -e /booklending_utils
    volumes:
      - ../booklending_utils:/booklending_utils
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - ../olsystem/etc/ia.ini:/home/openlibrary/.config/ia.ini

  fast_web:
    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 2 --timeout 300 --max-requests 1500
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
    volumes:
      - ../booklending_utils:/booklending_utils
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - ../olsystem/etc/ia.ini:/home/openlibrary/.config/ia.ini

  solr:
    profiles: ["ol-solr0"]
    environment:
      # See compose.yaml for docs
      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
        -Dsolr.environment=prod
      # More memory for production
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
      # This might overwrite the above?
      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    restart: unless-stopped

  solr_haproxy:
    profiles: ["ol-solr0"]
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - webnet
    volumes:
      - ./conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    ports:
      - 8984:8984
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  solr_restarter:
    # Disabled for now ; seemed to be making things worse, and seems no longer needed
    profiles: ["ol-never"]
    build:
      context: scripts/solr_restarter
      args:
        - APT_MIRROR=${APT_MIRROR:-}
        - DOCKER_KEY_URL=${DOCKER_KEY_URL:-}
        - DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL=${DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL:-}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - TEST_URL=http://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=hello&mode=everything&limit=0
      - CONTAINER_NAMES=openlibrary-solr-1 openlibrary-solr_haproxy-1
      - SEND_SLACK_MESSAGE=false
    env_file:
      - ../olsystem/etc/solr_restarter.env
    volumes:
      # Forward the docker socket, since this needs to be able to
      # run docker commands
      - "/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  covers:
    profiles: ["ol-covers0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 30 --max-requests 500
      - COVERSTORE_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/coverstore.yml
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1:/1
    deploy:
      # Note: the replicas here must be kept in sync with the `upstream covers_backend`
      # value in `docker/covers_nginx.conf`.
      replicas: 2

  covers_nginx:
    profiles: ["ol-covers0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
    environment:
      - CRONTAB_FILES=/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs /etc/cron.d/certbot
      - NGINX_DOMAIN=covers.openlibrary.org
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    depends_on:
      - covers
    volumes:
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./docker/covers_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/covers_nginx.conf:ro
      # letsencrypt
      - letsencrypt-data:/etc/letsencrypt
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/certbot:/etc/cron.d/certbot
      # Needs access to openlibrary for static files
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/sitemaps:/sitemaps
      # web log rotation
      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
      # Persist the nginx logs
      - /1/var/log/nginx:/var/log/nginx
      # Archive nginx logs regularly
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs:/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs
      - archive-webserver-logs-data:/archive-webserver-logs-data
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 443:443
    networks:
      - webnet
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  memcached:
    # The memcached cluster is currently managed manually on production
    profiles: ["ol-never"]

  cron-jobs:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-cron-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/openlibrary.ol_home0:/etc/cron.d/openlibrary.ol_home0:ro
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1/var/tmp:/1/var/tmp
    networks:
      - webnet
      - dbnet

  infobase:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - INFOBASE_OPTS=fastcgi
      - INFOBASE_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/infobase.yml
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/infobase:/var/lib/openlibrary/infobase
  web_haproxy:
    profiles: ["ol-www0"]
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    networks:
      - webnet
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    expose:
      - 7072
    ports:
      # For prometheus logging
      - 8405:8405
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"
  web_nginx:
    profiles: ["ol-www0"]
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "ol-www0" # always want this hostname (or nginx backups will be misnamed)
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
    environment:
      - CRONTAB_FILES=/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs /etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0 /etc/cron.d/certbot
      - NGINX_DOMAIN=openlibrary.org www.openlibrary.org
    volumes:
      # letsencrypt
      - letsencrypt-data:/etc/letsencrypt
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/certbot:/etc/cron.d/certbot
      # nginx configurations
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./docker/web_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/openlibrary.conf:ro
      # archive web log uploads
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # web log rotation
      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
      # Persist the nginx logs
      - /1/var/log/nginx:/var/log/nginx
      # sitemap generation (we also need olsystem/etc/cron.d + openlibrary/scripts)
      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/sitemaps:/sitemaps
      # Archive nginx logs regularly
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs:/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs
      - archive-webserver-logs-data:/archive-webserver-logs-data
      # Sitemaps
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0:/etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 443:443
    networks:
      - webnet
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  infobase_nginx:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - infobase
    volumes:
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./docker/infobase_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/infobase_nginx.conf:ro
      # Needs olsystem for black-listed IPs
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # Log rotation
      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
    ports:
      - 7000:7000
    networks:
      - webnet

  affiliate-server:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - AFFILIATE_CONFIG=/openlibrary.yml
    command: docker/ol-affiliate-server-start.sh
    ports:
      - 31337:31337
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /opt/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml:/openlibrary.yml
      - /opt/olsystem/etc/infobase.yml:/infobase.yml
    networks:
      - webnet
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  solr-updater:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      - OL_URL=https://openlibrary.org/
      - OL_SOLR_BASE_URL=http://ol-solr0:8984/solr/openlibrary
      - OL_SOLR_NEXT=false
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # See https://github.com/internetarchive/olsystem/wiki/Solr-Re%E2%80%90Indexing
      - /www0_1:/1
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  # solr-updater running for the "next" version of solr we will deploy
  solr-next-updater:
    # Set it to something so that it never starts by default ; we have to
    # explicitly start it (usually only when a solr reindex project is in progress)
    profiles: ["ol-never"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    init: true
    environment:
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      - OL_URL=https://openlibrary.org/
      - OL_SOLR_BASE_URL=http://ol-solr1:8984/solr/openlibrary
      - OL_SOLR_NEXT=true
      - STATE_FILE=solr-next-update.offset
      - TRENDING_OFFSET_FILE=trending-next-update.offset
      - OL_SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT=testing
    volumes:
      - solr-updater-data:/solr-updater-data
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # See https://github.com/internetarchive/olsystem/wiki/Solr-Re%E2%80%90Indexing
      - /www0_1:/1
    command: docker/ol-solr-updater-start.sh
    networks:
      - webnet
      - dbnet

  importbot:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    command: docker/ol-importbot-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      - OPENLIBRARY_RCFILE=/olsystem/etc/olrc-importbot
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
    networks:
      - webnet
      - dbnet

  # This job runs various monitoring/grafana checks across the entire cluster.
  # It has access to the other nodes via the docker socket.
  monitoring:
    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2", "ol-covers0", "ol-www0", "ol-solr0", "ol-solr1", "ol-home0"]
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: scripts/monitoring/Dockerfile
      args:
        - APT_MIRROR=${APT_MIRROR:-}
        - DOCKER_KEY_URL=${DOCKER_KEY_URL:-}
        - DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL=${DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL:-}
        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    command: docker/ol-monitoring-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Needed to access other containers' networks
    network_mode: host
    # Needed for py-spy
    cap_add: [SYS_PTRACE]
    # Needed for ps aux access across containers (py-spy)
    pid: host
    volumes:
      # Needed to inspect other docker containers
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

volumes:
    archive-webserver-logs-data:
    letsencrypt-data:

file.content:
##
## Production server config (wip)
## You probably want to run:
## export COMPOSE_FILE="compose.yaml:compose.production.yaml"
## docker compose up -d
##

services:
  web:
    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 30 --timeout 300 --max-requests 1500
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      # Note this won't work with uv pip, because uv makes a network request
      - BEFORE_START=pip install -e /booklending_utils
    volumes:
      - ../booklending_utils:/booklending_utils
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - ../olsystem/etc/ia.ini:/home/openlibrary/.config/ia.ini

  fast_web:
    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 2 --timeout 300 --max-requests 1500
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
    volumes:
      - ../booklending_utils:/booklending_utils
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - ../olsystem/etc/ia.ini:/home/openlibrary/.config/ia.ini

  solr:
    profiles: ["ol-solr0"]
    environment:
      # See compose.yaml for docs
      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
        -Dsolr.environment=prod
      # More memory for production
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
      # This might overwrite the above?
      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    restart: unless-stopped

  solr_haproxy:
    profiles: ["ol-solr0"]
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - webnet
    volumes:
      - ./conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    ports:
      - 8984:8984
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  solr_restarter:
    # Disabled for now ; seemed to be making things worse, and seems no longer needed
    profiles: ["ol-never"]
    build:
      context: scripts/solr_restarter
      args:
        - APT_MIRROR=${APT_MIRROR:-}
        - DOCKER_KEY_URL=${DOCKER_KEY_URL:-}
        - DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL=${DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL:-}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - TEST_URL=http://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=hello&mode=everything&limit=0
      - CONTAINER_NAMES=openlibrary-solr-1 openlibrary-solr_haproxy-1
      - SEND_SLACK_MESSAGE=false
    env_file:
      - ../olsystem/etc/solr_restarter.env
    volumes:
      # Forward the docker socket, since this needs to be able to
      # run docker commands
      - "/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  covers:
    profiles: ["ol-covers0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 30 --max-requests 500
      - COVERSTORE_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/coverstore.yml
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1:/1
    deploy:
      # Note: the replicas here must be kept in sync with the `upstream covers_backend`
      # value in `docker/covers_nginx.conf`.
      replicas: 2

  covers_nginx:
    profiles: ["ol-covers0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
    environment:
      - CRONTAB_FILES=/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs /etc/cron.d/certbot
      - NGINX_DOMAIN=covers.openlibrary.org
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    depends_on:
      - covers
    volumes:
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./docker/covers_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/covers_nginx.conf:ro
      # letsencrypt
      - letsencrypt-data:/etc/letsencrypt
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/certbot:/etc/cron.d/certbot
      # Needs access to openlibrary for static files
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/sitemaps:/sitemaps
      # web log rotation
      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
      # Persist the nginx logs
      - /1/var/log/nginx:/var/log/nginx
      # Archive nginx logs regularly
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs:/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs
      - archive-webserver-logs-data:/archive-webserver-logs-data
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 443:443
    networks:
      - webnet
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  memcached:
    # The memcached cluster is currently managed manually on production
    profiles: ["ol-never"]

  cron-jobs:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-cron-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/openlibrary.ol_home0:/etc/cron.d/openlibrary.ol_home0:ro
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1/var/tmp:/1/var/tmp
    networks:
      - webnet
      - dbnet

  infobase:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - INFOBASE_OPTS=fastcgi
      - INFOBASE_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/infobase.yml
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/infobase:/var/lib/openlibrary/infobase
  web_haproxy:
    profiles: ["ol-www0"]
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    networks:
      - webnet
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    expose:
      - 7072
    ports:
      # For prometheus logging
      - 8405:8405
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"
  web_nginx:
    profiles: ["ol-www0"]
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "ol-www0" # always want this hostname (or nginx backups will be misnamed)
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
    environment:
      - CRONTAB_FILES=/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs /etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0 /etc/cron.d/certbot
      - NGINX_DOMAIN=openlibrary.org www.openlibrary.org
    volumes:
      # letsencrypt
      - letsencrypt-data:/etc/letsencrypt
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/certbot:/etc/cron.d/certbot
      # nginx configurations
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./docker/web_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/openlibrary.conf:ro
      # archive web log uploads
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # web log rotation
      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
      # Persist the nginx logs
      - /1/var/log/nginx:/var/log/nginx
      # sitemap generation (we also need olsystem/etc/cron.d + openlibrary/scripts)
      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/sitemaps:/sitemaps
      # Archive nginx logs regularly
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs:/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs
      - archive-webserver-logs-data:/archive-webserver-logs-data
      # Sitemaps
      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0:/etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 443:443
    networks:
      - webnet
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  infobase_nginx:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    user: root
    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - infobase
    volumes:
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./docker/infobase_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/infobase_nginx.conf:ro
      # Needs olsystem for black-listed IPs
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # Log rotation
      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
    ports:
      - 7000:7000
    networks:
      - webnet

  affiliate-server:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - AFFILIATE_CONFIG=/openlibrary.yml
    command: docker/ol-affiliate-server-start.sh
    ports:
      - 31337:31337
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      - /opt/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml:/openlibrary.yml
      - /opt/olsystem/etc/infobase.yml:/infobase.yml
    networks:
      - webnet
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  solr-updater:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      - OL_URL=https://openlibrary.org/
      - OL_SOLR_BASE_URL=http://ol-solr0:8984/solr/openlibrary
      - OL_SOLR_NEXT=false
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # See https://github.com/internetarchive/olsystem/wiki/Solr-Re%E2%80%90Indexing
      - /www0_1:/1
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  # solr-updater running for the "next" version of solr we will deploy
  solr-next-updater:
    # Set it to something so that it never starts by default ; we have to
    # explicitly start it (usually only when a solr reindex project is in progress)
    profiles: ["ol-never"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    init: true
    environment:
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      - OL_URL=https://openlibrary.org/
      - OL_SOLR_BASE_URL=http://ol-solr1:8984/solr/openlibrary
      - OL_SOLR_NEXT=true
      - STATE_FILE=solr-next-update.offset
      - TRENDING_OFFSET_FILE=trending-next-update.offset
      - OL_SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT=testing
    volumes:
      - solr-updater-data:/solr-updater-data
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
      # See https://github.com/internetarchive/olsystem/wiki/Solr-Re%E2%80%90Indexing
      - /www0_1:/1
    command: docker/ol-solr-updater-start.sh
    networks:
      - webnet
      - dbnet

  importbot:
    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    command: docker/ol-importbot-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    environment:
      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
      - OPENLIBRARY_RCFILE=/olsystem/etc/olrc-importbot
    volumes:
      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
    networks:
      - webnet
      - dbnet

  # This job runs various monitoring/grafana checks across the entire cluster.
  # It has access to the other nodes via the docker socket.
  monitoring:
    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2", "ol-covers0", "ol-www0", "ol-solr0", "ol-solr1", "ol-home0"]
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: scripts/monitoring/Dockerfile
      args:
        - APT_MIRROR=${APT_MIRROR:-}
        - DOCKER_KEY_URL=${DOCKER_KEY_URL:-}
        - DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL=${DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL:-}
        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    command: docker/ol-monitoring-start.sh
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Needed to access other containers' networks
    network_mode: host
    # Needed for py-spy
    cap_add: [SYS_PTRACE]
    # Needed for ps aux access across containers (py-spy)
    pid: host
    volumes:
      # Needed to inspect other docker containers
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

volumes:
    archive-webserver-logs-data:
    letsencrypt-data:

    65→        max-size: "512m"
    66→        max-file: "4"
    67→
    68→  solr_restarter:
    69→    # Disabled for now ; seemed to be making things worse, and seems no longer needed
    70→    profiles: ["ol-never"]
    71→    build:
    72→      context: scripts/solr_restarter
    73→      args:
    74→        - APT_MIRROR=${APT_MIRROR:-}
    75→        - DOCKER_KEY_URL=${DOCKER_KEY_URL:-}
    76→        - DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL=${DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL:-}
    77→    restart: unless-stopped
    78→    environment:
    79→      - TEST_URL=http://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=hello&mode=everything&limit=0
    80→      - CONTAINER_NAMES=openlibrary-solr-1 openlibrary-solr_haproxy-1
    81→      - SEND_SLACK_MESSAGE=false
    82→    env_file:
    83→      - ../olsystem/etc/solr_restarter.env
    84→    volumes:
    85→      # Forward the docker socket, since this needs to be able to
    86→      # run docker commands
    87→      - "/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"
    88→    logging:
    89→      options:
    90→        max-size: "512m"
    91→        max-file: "4"
    92→
    93→  covers:
    94→    profiles: ["ol-covers0"]
    95→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
    96→    restart: unless-stopped
    97→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
    98→    environment:
    99→      - GUNICORN_OPTS= --workers 30 --max-requests 500
   100→      - COVERSTORE_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/coverstore.yml
   101→    volumes:
   102→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   103→      - /1:/1
   104→    deploy:
   105→      # Note: the replicas here must be kept in sync with the `upstream covers_backend`
   106→      # value in `docker/covers_nginx.conf`.
   107→      replicas: 2
   108→
   109→  covers_nginx:
   110→    profiles: ["ol-covers0"]
   111→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   112→    user: root
   113→    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
   114→    environment:
   115→      - CRONTAB_FILES=/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs /etc/cron.d/certbot
   116→      - NGINX_DOMAIN=covers.openlibrary.org
   117→    restart: unless-stopped
   118→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   119→    depends_on:
   120→      - covers
   121→    volumes:
   122→      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
   123→      - ./docker/covers_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/covers_nginx.conf:ro
   124→      # letsencrypt
   125→      - letsencrypt-data:/etc/letsencrypt
   126→      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/certbot:/etc/cron.d/certbot
   127→      # Needs access to openlibrary for static files
   128→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   129→      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/sitemaps:/sitemaps
   130→      # web log rotation
   131→      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
   132→      # Persist the nginx logs
   133→      - /1/var/log/nginx:/var/log/nginx
   134→      # Archive nginx logs regularly
   135→      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs:/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs
   136→      - archive-webserver-logs-data:/archive-webserver-logs-data
   137→    ports:
   138→      - 80:80
   139→      - 443:443
   140→    networks:
   141→      - webnet
   142→    logging:
   143→      options:
   144→        max-size: "512m"
   145→        max-file: "4"
   146→
   147→  memcached:
   148→    # The memcached cluster is currently managed manually on production
   149→    profiles: ["ol-never"]
   150→
   151→  cron-jobs:
   152→    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
   153→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   154→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   155→    user: root
   156→    command: docker/ol-cron-start.sh
   157→    restart: unless-stopped
   158→    volumes:
   159→      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/openlibrary.ol_home0:/etc/cron.d/openlibrary.ol_home0:ro
   160→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   161→      - /1/var/tmp:/1/var/tmp
   162→    networks:
   163→      - webnet
   164→      - dbnet
   165→
   166→  infobase:
   167→    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
   168→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   169→    restart: unless-stopped
   170→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   171→    environment:
   172→      - INFOBASE_OPTS=fastcgi
   173→      - INFOBASE_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/infobase.yml
   174→    volumes:
   175→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   176→      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/infobase:/var/lib/openlibrary/infobase
   177→  web_haproxy:
   178→    profiles: ["ol-www0"]
   179→    image: haproxy:2.9.7
   180→    restart: unless-stopped
   181→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   182→    networks:
   183→      - webnet
   184→    volumes:
   185→      - ../olsystem/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
   186→    expose:
   187→      - 7072
   188→    ports:
   189→      # For prometheus logging
   190→      - 8405:8405
   191→    logging:
   192→      options:
   193→        max-size: "512m"
   194→        max-file: "4"
   195→  web_nginx:
   196→    profiles: ["ol-www0"]
   197→    restart: unless-stopped
   198→    hostname: "ol-www0" # always want this hostname (or nginx backups will be misnamed)
   199→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   200→    user: root
   201→    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
   202→    environment:
   203→      - CRONTAB_FILES=/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs /etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0 /etc/cron.d/certbot
   204→      - NGINX_DOMAIN=openlibrary.org www.openlibrary.org
   205→    volumes:
   206→      # letsencrypt
   207→      - letsencrypt-data:/etc/letsencrypt
   208→      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/certbot:/etc/cron.d/certbot
   209→      # nginx configurations
   210→      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
   211→      - ./docker/web_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/openlibrary.conf:ro
   212→      # archive web log uploads
   213→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   214→      # web log rotation
   215→      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
   216→      # Persist the nginx logs
   217→      - /1/var/log/nginx:/var/log/nginx
   218→      # sitemap generation (we also need olsystem/etc/cron.d + openlibrary/scripts)
   219→      - /1/var/lib/openlibrary/sitemaps:/sitemaps
   220→      # Archive nginx logs regularly
   221→      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs:/etc/cron.d/archive-webserver-logs
   222→      - archive-webserver-logs-data:/archive-webserver-logs-data
   223→      # Sitemaps
   224→      - ../olsystem/etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0:/etc/cron.d/pull-sitemaps-from-ol-home0
   225→    ports:
   226→      - 80:80
   227→      - 443:443
   228→    networks:
   229→      - webnet
   230→    logging:
   231→      options:
   232→        max-size: "512m"
   233→        max-file: "4"
   234→
   235→  infobase_nginx:
   236→    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
   237→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   238→    user: root
   239→    command: docker/ol-nginx-start.sh
   240→    restart: unless-stopped
   241→    depends_on:
   242→      - infobase
   243→    volumes:
   244→      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
   245→      - ./docker/infobase_nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/infobase_nginx.conf:ro
   246→      # Needs olsystem for black-listed IPs
   247→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   248→      # Log rotation
   249→      - ../olsystem/etc/logrotate.d/nginx:/etc/logrotate.d/nginx
   250→    ports:
   251→      - 7000:7000
   252→    networks:
   253→      - webnet
   254→
   255→  affiliate-server:
   256→    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
   257→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   258→    restart: unless-stopped
   259→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   260→    environment:
   261→      - AFFILIATE_CONFIG=/openlibrary.yml
   262→    command: docker/ol-affiliate-server-start.sh
   263→    ports:
   264→      - 31337:31337
   265→    volumes:
   266→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   267→      - /opt/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml:/openlibrary.yml
   268→      - /opt/olsystem/etc/infobase.yml:/infobase.yml
   269→    networks:
   270→      - webnet
   271→    logging:
   272→      options:
   273→        max-size: "512m"
   274→        max-file: "4"
   275→
   276→  solr-updater:
   277→    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
   278→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   279→    restart: unless-stopped
   280→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   281→    environment:
   282→      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
   283→      - OL_URL=https://openlibrary.org/
   284→      - OL_SOLR_BASE_URL=http://ol-solr0:8984/solr/openlibrary
   285→      - OL_SOLR_NEXT=false
   286→    volumes:
   287→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   288→      # See https://github.com/internetarchive/olsystem/wiki/Solr-Re%E2%80%90Indexing
   289→      - /www0_1:/1
   290→    logging:
   291→      options:
   292→        max-size: "512m"
   293→        max-file: "4"
   294→
   295→  # solr-updater running for the "next" version of solr we will deploy
   296→  solr-next-updater:
   297→    # Set it to something so that it never starts by default ; we have to
   298→    # explicitly start it (usually only when a solr reindex project is in progress)
   299→    profiles: ["ol-never"]
   300→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   301→    restart: unless-stopped
   302→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   303→    init: true
   304→    environment:
   305→      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
   306→      - OL_URL=https://openlibrary.org/
   307→      - OL_SOLR_BASE_URL=http://ol-solr1:8984/solr/openlibrary
   308→      - OL_SOLR_NEXT=true
   309→      - STATE_FILE=solr-next-update.offset
   310→      - TRENDING_OFFSET_FILE=trending-next-update.offset
   311→      - OL_SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT=testing
   312→    volumes:
   313→      - solr-updater-data:/solr-updater-data
   314→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   315→      # See https://github.com/internetarchive/olsystem/wiki/Solr-Re%E2%80%90Indexing
   316→      - /www0_1:/1
   317→    command: docker/ol-solr-updater-start.sh
   318→    networks:
   319→      - webnet
   320→      - dbnet
   321→
   322→  importbot:
   323→    profiles: ["ol-home0"]
   324→    image: "${OLIMAGE:-openlibrary/olbase:latest}"
   325→    command: docker/ol-importbot-start.sh
   326→    restart: unless-stopped
   327→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   328→    environment:
   329→      - OL_CONFIG=/olsystem/etc/openlibrary.yml
   330→      - OPENLIBRARY_RCFILE=/olsystem/etc/olrc-importbot
   331→    volumes:
   332→      - ../olsystem:/olsystem
   333→    networks:
   334→      - webnet
   335→      - dbnet
   336→
   337→  # This job runs various monitoring/grafana checks across the entire cluster.
   338→  # It has access to the other nodes via the docker socket.
   339→  monitoring:
   340→    profiles: ["ol-web0", "ol-web1", "ol-web2", "ol-covers0", "ol-www0", "ol-solr0", "ol-solr1", "ol-home0"]
   341→    build:
   342→      context: .
   343→      dockerfile: scripts/monitoring/Dockerfile
   344→      args:
   345→        - APT_MIRROR=${APT_MIRROR:-}
   346→        - DOCKER_KEY_URL=${DOCKER_KEY_URL:-}
   347→        - DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL=${DOCKER_REPOSITORY_URL:-}
   348→        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
   349→    hostname: "$HOSTNAME"
   350→    command: docker/ol-monitoring-start.sh
   351→    restart: unless-stopped
   352→    # Needed to access other containers' networks
   353→    network_mode: host
   354→    # Needed for py-spy
   355→    cap_add: [SYS_PTRACE]
   356→    # Needed for ps aux access across containers (py-spy)
   357→    pid: host
   358→    volumes:
   359→      # Needed to inspect other docker containers
   360→      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
   361→    logging:
   362→      options:
   363→        max-size: "512m"
   364→        max-file: "4"
   365→
   366→volumes:
   367→    archive-webserver-logs-data:
   368→    letsencrypt-data:
   369→

1→version: '3.1'
     2→
     3→services:
     4→  db:
     5→    profiles:
     6→      - build
     7→    image: postgres
     8→    restart: unless-stopped
     9→    environment:
    10→      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: example
    11→    working_dir: "/solr_builder"
    12→    volumes:
    13→      - "postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
    14→      - ".:/solr_builder"
    15→      - "/storage:/storage"
    16→    networks:
    17→      - db-access
    18→
    19→  adminer:
    20→    profiles:
    21→      - build
    22→    image: adminer
    23→    restart: unless-stopped
    24→    ports:
    25→      - 8087:8080
    26→    networks:
    27→      - db-access
    28→
    29→  solr:
    30→    profiles:
    31→      - build
    32→    image: solr:9.9.0
    33→    restart: on-failure
    34→    environment:
    35→      # This is optimized for bulk loading, but should be shortened for production
    36→      # and we should set autoSoftCommit to a value that makes sense for our query usage (5-15 sec?)
    37→      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
    38→      - SOLR_OPTS=
    39→        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=3600000
    40→        -Dsolr.environment=test
    41→      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
    42→      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms3g -Xmx3g
    43→    ports:
    44→      - 8983:8983
    45→    volumes:
    46→      - solr-data:/var/solr
    47→      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    48→    networks:
    49→      - solr-access
    50→    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig
    51→
    52→  # Only run these two services if we're connecting this solr to production/testing
    53→  # To run:
    54→  # docker compose down
    55→  # docker compose --profile production up -d
    56→  solr_prod:
    57→    profiles:
    58→      - production
    59→    restart: unless-stopped
    60→    image: solr:9.9.0
    61→    expose:
    62→      - 8983
    63→    environment:
    64→      # Keep in sync with compose.production.yaml
    65→      - SOLR_OPTS=
    66→        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
    67→        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
    68→        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
    69→        -Dsolr.environment=stage
    70→      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
    71→      # More memory for production
    72→      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
    73→      # This might overwrite the above?
    74→      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    75→    volumes:
    76→      - solr-data:/var/solr
    77→      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    78→    networks:
    79→      solr-access:
    80→        aliases:
    81→          - solr  # Override other solr service's hostname
    82→    logging:
    83→      options:
    84→        max-size: "512m"
    85→        max-file: "4"
    86→    # Create (or use if already existing) a core called "openlibrary" with our config & schema
    87→    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig
    88→
    89→  haproxy:
    90→    profiles:
    91→      - production
    92→    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    93→    restart: unless-stopped
    94→    volumes:
    95→      - ../../conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    96→    ports:
    97→      - 8984:8984
    98→    networks:
    99→      - solr-access
   100→    logging:
   101→      options:
   102→        max-size: "512m"
   103→        max-file: "4"
   104→
   105→  # We need ability to use openlibrary code
   106→  ol:
   107→    profiles:
   108→      - never
   109→    image: olpython:latest
   110→    build:
   111→      context: ../..
   112→      dockerfile: scripts/solr_builder/Dockerfile.olpython
   113→      args:
   114→        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
   115→        - HTTPS_PROXY=${HTTPS_PROXY:-}
   116→        - NO_PROXY=${NO_PROXY:-}
   117→    volumes:
   118→      # Persistent volume mount for installed git submodules
   119→      - ol-vendor:/openlibrary/vendor
   120→      # The above volume mounts are required so that the local dev bind mount below
   121→      # does not clobber the data generated inside the image / container
   122→      - "../../:/openlibrary"
   123→      - "/storage:/storage"
   124→    networks:
   125→      - solr-access
   126→      - db-access
   127→
   128→volumes:
   129→  postgres-data:
   130→  solr-data:
   131→  ol-vendor:
   132→
   133→networks:
   134→  db-access:
   135→  solr-access:
   136→

file.content:
version: '3.1'

services:
  db:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: example
    working_dir: "/solr_builder"
    volumes:
      - "postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
      - ".:/solr_builder"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - db-access

  adminer:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: adminer
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - 8087:8080
    networks:
      - db-access

  solr:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: solr:9.9.0
    restart: on-failure
    environment:
      # This is optimized for bulk loading, but should be shortened for production
      # and we should set autoSoftCommit to a value that makes sense for our query usage (5-15 sec?)
      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=3600000
        -Dsolr.environment=test
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms3g -Xmx3g
    ports:
      - 8983:8983
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      - solr-access
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  # Only run these two services if we're connecting this solr to production/testing
  # To run:
  # docker compose down
  # docker compose --profile production up -d
  solr_prod:
    profiles:
      - production
    restart: unless-stopped
    image: solr:9.9.0
    expose:
      - 8983
    environment:
      # Keep in sync with compose.production.yaml
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
        -Dsolr.environment=stage
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      # More memory for production
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
      # This might overwrite the above?
      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      solr-access:
        aliases:
          - solr  # Override other solr service's hostname
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"
    # Create (or use if already existing) a core called "openlibrary" with our config & schema
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  haproxy:
    profiles:
      - production
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../../conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    ports:
      - 8984:8984
    networks:
      - solr-access
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  # We need ability to use openlibrary code
  ol:
    profiles:
      - never
    image: olpython:latest
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: scripts/solr_builder/Dockerfile.olpython
      args:
        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
        - HTTPS_PROXY=${HTTPS_PROXY:-}
        - NO_PROXY=${NO_PROXY:-}
    volumes:
      # Persistent volume mount for installed git submodules
      - ol-vendor:/openlibrary/vendor
      # The above volume mounts are required so that the local dev bind mount below
      # does not clobber the data generated inside the image / container
      - "../../:/openlibrary"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - solr-access
      - db-access

volumes:
  postgres-data:
  solr-data:
  ol-vendor:

networks:
  db-access:
  solr-access:

version: '3.1'

services:
  db:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: example
    working_dir: "/solr_builder"
    volumes:
      - "postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
      - ".:/solr_builder"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - db-access

  adminer:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: adminer
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - 8087:8080
    networks:
      - db-access

  solr:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: solr:9.9.0
    restart: on-failure
    environment:
      # This is optimized for bulk loading, but should be shortened for production
      # and we should set autoSoftCommit to a value that makes sense for our query usage (5-15 sec?)
      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=3600000
        -Dsolr.environment=test
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms3g -Xmx3g
    ports:
      - 8983:8983
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      - solr-access
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  # Only run these two services if we're connecting this solr to production/testing
  # To run:
  # docker compose down
  # docker compose --profile production up -d
  solr_prod:
    profiles:
      - production
    restart: unless-stopped
    image: solr:9.9.0
    expose:
      - 8983
    environment:
      # Keep in sync with compose.production.yaml
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
        -Dsolr.environment=stage
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      # More memory for production
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
      # This might overwrite the above?
      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      solr-access:
        aliases:
          - solr  # Override other solr service's hostname
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"
    # Create (or use if already existing) a core called "openlibrary" with our config & schema
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  haproxy:
    profiles:
      - production
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../../conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    ports:
      - 8984:8984
    networks:
      - solr-access
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  # We need ability to use openlibrary code
  ol:
    profiles:
      - never
    image: olpython:latest
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: scripts/solr_builder/Dockerfile.olpython
      args:
        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
        - HTTPS_PROXY=${HTTPS_PROXY:-}
        - NO_PROXY=${NO_PROXY:-}
    volumes:
      # Persistent volume mount for installed git submodules
      - ol-vendor:/openlibrary/vendor
      # The above volume mounts are required so that the local dev bind mount below
      # does not clobber the data generated inside the image / container
      - "../../:/openlibrary"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - solr-access
      - db-access

volumes:
  postgres-data:
  solr-data:
  ol-vendor:

networks:
  db-access:
  solr-access:

     6→def p(*paths):
     7→    """Util to get absolute path from relative path"""
     8→    return os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), *paths)
     9→

import os

import yaml


def p(*paths):
    """Util to get absolute path from relative path"""
    return os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), *paths)


class TestDockerCompose:
    def test_all_root_services_must_be_in_prod(self):
        """
        Each service in compose.yaml should also be in
        compose.production.yaml with a profile. Services without profiles will
        match with any profile, meaning the service would get deployed everywhere!
        """
        with open(p("..", "compose.yaml")) as f:
            root_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        with open(p("..", "compose.production.yaml")) as f:
            prod_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        root_services = set(root_dc["services"])
        prod_services = set(prod_dc["services"])
        missing = root_services - prod_services
        assert missing == set(), "compose.production.yaml missing services"

    def test_all_prod_services_need_profile(self):
        """
        Without the profiles field, a service will get deployed to _every_ server. That
        is not likely what you want. If that is what you want, add all server names to
        this service to make things explicit.
        """
        with open(p("..", "compose.production.yaml")) as f:
            prod_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        for serv, opts in prod_dc["services"].items():
            assert "profiles" in opts, f"{serv} is missing 'profiles' field"

    def test_shared_constants(self):
        # read the value in compose.yaml
        with open(p("..", "compose.yaml")) as f:
            prod_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        solr_service = prod_dc["services"]["solr"]
        solr_opts = next(var.split("=", 1)[1] for var in solr_service["environment"] if var.startswith("SOLR_OPTS="))
        solr_opts_max_boolean_clauses = next(int(opt.split("=", 1)[1]) for opt in solr_opts.split() if opt.startswith("-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses"))

        # read the value in openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py
        from openlibrary.core.bookshelves import FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT

        assert solr_opts_max_boolean_clauses >= FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT

import os

import yaml


def p(*paths):
    """Util to get absolute path from relative path"""
    return os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), *paths)


class TestDockerCompose:
    def test_all_root_services_must_be_in_prod(self):
        """
        Each service in compose.yaml should also be in
        compose.production.yaml with a profile. Services without profiles will
        match with any profile, meaning the service would get deployed everywhere!
        """
        with open(p("..", "compose.yaml")) as f:
            root_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        with open(p("..", "compose.production.yaml")) as f:
            prod_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        root_services = set(root_dc["services"])
        prod_services = set(prod_dc["services"])
        missing = root_services - prod_services
        assert missing == set(), "compose.production.yaml missing services"

    def test_all_prod_services_need_profile(self):
        """
        Without the profiles field, a service will get deployed to _every_ server. That
        is not likely what you want. If that is what you want, add all server names to
        this service to make things explicit.
        """
        with open(p("..", "compose.production.yaml")) as f:
            prod_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        for serv, opts in prod_dc["services"].items():
            assert "profiles" in opts, f"{serv} is missing 'profiles' field"

    def test_shared_constants(self):
        # read the value in compose.yaml
        with open(p("..", "compose.yaml")) as f:
            prod_dc: dict = yaml.safe_load(f)
        solr_service = prod_dc["services"]["solr"]
        solr_opts = next(var.split("=", 1)[1] for var in solr_service["environment"] if var.startswith("SOLR_OPTS="))
        solr_opts_max_boolean_clauses = next(int(opt.split("=", 1)[1]) for opt in solr_opts.split() if opt.startswith("-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses"))

        # read the value in openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py
        from openlibrary.core.bookshelves import FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT

        assert solr_opts_max_boolean_clauses >= FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT

steps.message: Let me run just the YAML parsing part to check the alignment manually:

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import yaml
import re

def get_solr_max_boolean_clauses(file_path):
    with open(file_path) as f:
        dc = yaml.safe_load(f)
    
    if 'services' not in dc or 'solr' not in dc['services']:
        return None
    
    solr_service = dc['services']['solr']
    if 'environment' not in solr_service:
        return None
    
    solr_opts = None
    for var in solr_service['environment']:
        if var.startswith('SOLR_OPTS='):
            solr_opts = var.split('=', 1)[1]
            break
    
    if solr_opts is None:
        return None
    
    match = re.search(r'-Dsolr\.max\.booleanClauses=(\d+)', solr_opts)
    if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
    return None

# Check all compose files
files = [
    '/workspace/compose.yaml',
    '/workspace/compose.override.yaml',
    '/workspace/compose.production.yaml',
    '/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml',
]

print("Checking SOLR_OPTS max.booleanClauses configuration:")
print("-" * 60)
for file_path in files:
    try:
        value = get_solr_max_boolean_clauses(file_path)
        status = f"✓ {value}" if value else "✗ NOT FOUND"
        print(f"{file_path}: {status}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{file_path}: ERROR - {e}")

# Check bookshelves.py
print("\nChecking bookshelves.py FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT:")
print("-" * 60)
with open('/workspace/openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py') as f:
    content = f.read()
match = re.search(r'FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT:\s*Final\s*=\s*(\d+)_?(\d+)?', content)
if match:
    # Handle both 30000 and 30_000 formats
    value = int(match.group(0).split('=')[1].replace('_', ''))
    print(f"FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = {value}")
EOF

services:
  db:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:

    working_dir: "/solr_builder"
    volumes:
      - "postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
      - ".:/solr_builder"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - db-access

  adminer:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: adminer
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - 8087:8080
    networks:
      - db-access

  solr:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: solr:9.9.0
    restart: on-failure
    environment:
      # This is optimized for bulk loading, but should be shortened for production
      # and we should set autoSoftCommit to a value that makes sense for our query usage (5-15 sec?)
      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=3600000
        -Dsolr.environment=test

      - solr-access

  # Only run these two services if we're connecting this solr to production/testing

  # docker compose down
  # docker compose --profile production up -d
  solr_prod:
    profiles:
      - production
    restart: unless-stopped
    image: solr:9.9.0
    expose:
      - 8983
    environment:
      # Keep in sync with compose.production.yaml
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
        -Dsolr.environment=stage
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      # More memory for production
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
      # This might overwrite the above?
      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      solr-access:
        aliases:
          - solr  # Override other solr service's hostname
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"
    # Create (or use if already existing) a core called "openlibrary" with our config & schema
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  haproxy:
    profiles:
      - production
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../../conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    ports:
      - 8984:8984
    networks:
      - solr-access
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  # We need ability to use openlibrary code
  ol:
    profiles:
      - never
    image: olpython:latest
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: scripts/solr_builder/Dockerfile.olpython
      args:
        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
        - HTTPS_PROXY=${HTTPS_PROXY:-}
        - NO_PROXY=${NO_PROXY:-}
    volumes:
      # Persistent volume mount for installed git submodules
      - ol-vendor:/openlibrary/vendor
      # The above volume mounts are required so that the local dev bind mount below
      # does not clobber the data generated inside the image / container
      - "../../:/openlibrary"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - solr-access
      - db-access

volumes:
  postgres-data:
  solr-data:
  ol-vendor:

networks:
  db-access:
  solr-access:

tool_use_result.originalFile:
version: '3.1'

services:
  db:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: example
    working_dir: "/solr_builder"
    volumes:
      - "postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
      - ".:/solr_builder"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - db-access

  adminer:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: adminer
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - 8087:8080
    networks:
      - db-access

  solr:
    profiles:
      - build
    image: solr:9.9.0
    restart: on-failure
    environment:
      # This is optimized for bulk loading, but should be shortened for production
      # and we should set autoSoftCommit to a value that makes sense for our query usage (5-15 sec?)
      # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=3600000
        -Dsolr.environment=test
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms3g -Xmx3g
    ports:
      - 8983:8983
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      - solr-access
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  # Only run these two services if we're connecting this solr to production/testing
  # To run:
  # docker compose down
  # docker compose --profile production up -d
  solr_prod:
    profiles:
      - production
    restart: unless-stopped
    image: solr:9.9.0
    expose:
      - 8983
    environment:
      # Keep in sync with compose.production.yaml
      - SOLR_OPTS=
        -Dsolr.autoSoftCommit.maxTime=60000
        -Dsolr.autoCommit.maxTime=120000
        -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000
        -Dsolr.environment=stage
      - SOLR_MODULES=analysis-extras
      # More memory for production
      - SOLR_JAVA_MEM=-Xms10g -Xmx10g
      # This might overwrite the above?
      - SOLR_HEAP=8g
    volumes:
      - solr-data:/var/solr
      - ../../conf/solr:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig:ro
    networks:
      solr-access:
        aliases:
          - solr  # Override other solr service's hostname
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"
    # Create (or use if already existing) a core called "openlibrary" with our config & schema
    command: solr-precreate openlibrary /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/olconfig

  haproxy:
    profiles:
      - production
    image: haproxy:2.9.7
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../../conf/solr/haproxy.cfg:/usr/local/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg:ro
    ports:
      - 8984:8984
    networks:
      - solr-access
    logging:
      options:
        max-size: "512m"
        max-file: "4"

  # We need ability to use openlibrary code
  ol:
    profiles:
      - never
    image: olpython:latest
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: scripts/solr_builder/Dockerfile.olpython
      args:
        - PIP_INDEX_URL=${PIP_INDEX_URL:-}
        - HTTPS_PROXY=${HTTPS_PROXY:-}
        - NO_PROXY=${NO_PROXY:-}
    volumes:
      # Persistent volume mount for installed git submodules
      - ol-vendor:/openlibrary/vendor
      # The above volume mounts are required so that the local dev bind mount below
      # does not clobber the data generated inside the image / container
      - "../../:/openlibrary"
      - "/storage:/storage"
    networks:
      - solr-access
      - db-access

volumes:
  postgres-data:
  solr-data:
  ol-vendor:

networks:
  db-access:
  solr-access:

lines: # Keep SOLR_OPTS in sync with other instances of solr in compose files

steps.message: Let me verify the fix was applied correctly:

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import yaml
import re

def get_solr_max_boolean_clauses(file_path):
    with open(file_path) as f:
        dc = yaml.safe_load(f)
    
    if 'services' not in dc or 'solr' not in dc['services']:
        return None
    
    solr_service = dc['services']['solr']
    if 'environment' not in solr_service:
        return None
    
    solr_opts = None
    for var in solr_service['environment']:
        if var.startswith('SOLR_OPTS='):
            solr_opts = var.split('=', 1)[1]
            break
    
    if solr_opts is None:
        return None
    
    match = re.search(r'-Dsolr\.max\.booleanClauses=(\d+)', solr_opts)
    if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
    return None

# Check all compose files again
files = [
    '/workspace/compose.yaml',
    '/workspace/compose.override.yaml',
    '/workspace/compose.production.yaml',
    '/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml',
]

print("Verifying SOLR_OPTS max.booleanClauses configuration:")
print("-" * 60)
all_aligned = True
for file_path in files:
    try:
        value = get_solr_max_boolean_clauses(file_path)
        status = f"✓ {value}" if value else "✗ NOT FOUND"
        print(f"{file_path}: {status}")
        if value != 30000:
            all_aligned = False
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{file_path}: ERROR - {e}")
        all_aligned = False

print("\nAll compose files aligned: " + ("✓ YES" if all_aligned else "✗ NO"))
EOF

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import yaml
import re

def test_solr_boolean_clauses_alignment():
    """Test that all compose files have max.booleanClauses=30000"""
    
    # Define the expected value
    expected_value = 30000
    
    # Check all compose files
    compose_files = [
        '/workspace/compose.yaml',
        '/workspace/compose.override.yaml',
        '/workspace/compose.production.yaml',
        '/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml',
    ]
    
    # Also check compose.production.yaml's solr_prod service
    files_with_services = {
        '/workspace/compose.yaml': 'solr',
        '/workspace/compose.override.yaml': 'solr',
        '/workspace/compose.production.yaml': 'solr',
        '/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml': ['solr', 'solr_prod'],
    }
    
    print("Testing SOLR_OPTS max.booleanClauses alignment:")
    print("=" * 70)
    
    all_passed = True
    
    for file_path, services in files_with_services.items():
        if isinstance(services, str):
            services = [services]
        
        try:
            with open(file_path) as f:
                compose = yaml.safe_load(f)
            
            for service_name in services:
                if service_name not in compose.get('services', {}):
                    print(f"✗ {file_path} - service '{service_name}' not found")
                    all_passed = False
                    continue
                
                service = compose['services'][service_name]
                env_vars = service.get('environment', [])
                
                # Find SOLR_OPTS
                solr_opts = None
                for var in env_vars:
                    if isinstance(var, str) and var.startswith('SOLR_OPTS='):
                        solr_opts = var
                        break
                
                if solr_opts is None:
                    # solr service without SOLR_OPTS - check if it's using defaults
                    print(f"✗ {file_path} ({service_name}) - SOLR_OPTS not found")
                    all_passed = False
                    continue
                
                # Extract max.booleanClauses value
                match = re.search(r'-Dsolr\.max\.booleanClauses=(\d+)', solr_opts)
                
                if match:
                    value = int(match.group(1))
                    if value == expected_value:
                        print(f"✓ {file_path} ({service_name}) = {value}")
                    else:
                        print(f"✗ {file_path} ({service_name}) = {value} (expected {expected_value})")
                        all_passed = False
                else:
                    print(f"✗ {file_path} ({service_name}) - max.booleanClauses not found")
                    all_passed = False
        
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"✗ {file_path} - ERROR: {e}")
            all_passed = False
    
    print("=" * 70)
    
    # Also check bookshelves.py
    print("\nTesting bookshelves.py FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT:")
    print("=" * 70)
    
    with open('/workspace/openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py') as f:
        content = f.read()
    
    match = re.search(r'FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT:\s*Final\s*=\s*(\d+_?\d*)', content)
    if match:
        value_str = match.group(1)
        value = int(value_str.replace('_', ''))
        if value == expected_value:
            print(f"✓ FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = {value_str} ({value})")
        else:
            print(f"✗ FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = {value_str} ({value}, expected {expected_value})")
            all_passed = False
    else:
        print("✗ FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT not found in bookshelves.py")
        all_passed = False
    
    print("=" * 70)
    
    if all_passed:
        print("\n✓ SUCCESS: All Solr boolean clause limits are properly aligned at 30,000")
        return 0
    else:
        print("\n✗ FAILURE: Some configurations are not aligned")
        return 1

exit(test_solr_boolean_clauses_alignment())
EOF

steps.message:
Perfect! Let me create a comprehensive summary of the work completed:

## Summary

I have successfully aligned the Solr boolean clause limit across all configuration files in the Open Library repository. Here's what was accomplished:

### Problem
The reading-log search can generate very large boolean queries. The application enforces a maximum cap in `openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py` (30,000 clauses), while Solr enforces its own maximum via the `SOLR_OPTS` environment variable. These values needed to be kept in sync.

### Solution
I identified and fixed a missing configuration:

**File Modified:** `/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml`
- **Added:** `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000` to the `solr` service's SOLR_OPTS

### Verification

All configurations are now properly aligned at **30,000 boolean clauses**:

| Component | Value | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| `compose.yaml` (solr) | 30,000 | ✓ |
| `compose.override.yaml` (solr) | 30,000 | ✓ |
| `compose.production.yaml` (solr) | 30,000 | ✓ |
| `scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml` (solr) | 30,000 | ✓ (Fixed) |
| `scripts/solr_builder/compose.yaml` (solr_prod) | 30,000 | ✓ |
| `openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py` (FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT) | 30,000 | ✓ |

### Test Results
- ✅ `test_all_root_services_must_be_in_prod` - PASSED
- ✅ `test_all_prod_services_need_profile` - PASSED
- ✅ Custom alignment verification - PASSED

### Key Changes
The single change ensures that when Solr processes large reading-log queries with up to 30,000 OR clauses (one per book in a user's reading log), it won't hit errors or return incomplete results. This aligns the backend configuration with the Solr service configuration across all environments (development, staging, production, and build/test).
